objective: Add the first governance-owned, repo-side pull verification slice so governed repos can verify pinned governance package consumption without being operator-pushed or self-mutating central GitHub rules.
scope_boundary: Document the consumer-pull architecture and mutation boundary.
Add a non-mutating verifier for downstream consumer records and managed files.
Add a machine-readable desired-state contract for the initial consumer-pull package surface.
Wire focused verifier tests into the governance package Local CI Gate surface.
out_of_scope: Applying or changing org rulesets, repo rulesets, required checks, bypass actors, or repository settings.
Replacing the existing governance package deployer.
Rolling the verifier into every governed repo in this slice.
research_summary: Existing issue #288/#290/#292/#338 work established a governance package, managed deployer, mandatory SHA pinning, consumer record path, and downstream adoption proof. This issue extends that package with a pull-side verifier so downstream repos can prove their own pinned consumption while central GitHub policy remains governed by explicit apply work.

sprints.name: Consumer pull verifier
sprints.goal: Let a consumer repo verify that its local consumer record and managed files match an exact pinned governance package ref.
tasks: Update package manifest, runbook, Local CI profile, and roadmap mirrors.
Run local gates and publish PR.
acceptance_criteria: Verifier exits 0 for a valid exact-SHA consumer record and managed shim.
Verifier exits non-zero for missing records, non-SHA refs, marker drift, profile mismatch, and missing managed files.
Runbook clearly distinguishes repo-pulled checks from centrally applied GitHub settings/rulesets.
Local CI Gate passes with the verifier tests included in package-tooling coverage.

specialist_reviews.focus: Keep consumer verification non-mutating and rollback-safe.
specialist_reviews.summary: The verifier only reads the target repo, package manifest, and desired-state file; it reports central GitHub settings as drift-only and does not apply them.

alternate_model_review.summary: The operator requested PDCAR and implementation of the consumer-pull architecture without HITL pauses.

execution_handoff.approved_scope_summary: Issue #398 may add planning artifacts, package contract docs, a non-mutating verifier, focused tests, Local CI profile coverage, and roadmap mirrors. It may not mutate downstream repos or GitHub rules/settings.
execution_handoff.next_execution_step: Run focused tests and Local CI Gate, then publish a PR.

material_deviation_rules: If implementation needs to mutate downstream repos, create downstream issue-backed work instead.
If implementation needs to change GitHub org/repo settings or rulesets, create a separate central apply issue with rollback evidence.
If package verification cannot distinguish repo-pulled from centrally applied surfaces, stop and keep the change in planning only.
